title: Masking (Causal & Padding)
summary: Causal mask sets upper triangle of attention scores to $-\infty$ before softmax, preventing future-token leakage. Enables parallel training of autoregressive models.
key_insights: Without masking, decoder can cheat by looking at future tokens during training
Set $S[i,j] = -\infty$ for $j > i$ before softmax. $e^{-\infty} = 0$ → future positions get zero weight
Lower triangular pattern: pos 1 sees only itself, pos 2 sees 1–2, etc.
Enables full sequence in ONE forward pass while causally correct
Encoder: NO causal mask. Cross-attention: NO mask.

mathematical_core.causal_mask: $M[i,j] = \begin{cases} 0 & j \leq i \\ -\infty & j > i \end{cases}$. Applied: $S_{\text{masked}} = S + M$
mathematical_core.padding_mask: $M[i,j] = \begin{cases} 0 & \text{token } j \text{ is real} \\ -\infty & \text{token } j \text{ is padding} \end{cases}$

derivations.title: Why $-\infty$ and not $0$

steps.name: Setting score to 0
steps.math: $\text{softmax}([s_1, s_2, 0]) = [e^{s_1}, e^{s_2}, 1]/Z$. Masked position gets weight $1/Z > 0$. Information leaks!

steps.name: Setting score to $-\infty$
steps.math: $\text{softmax}([s_1, s_2, -\infty]) = [e^{s_1}, e^{s_2}, 0]/Z$. Exactly zero. No leakage.

steps.name: In practice
steps.math: Use $-10^9$ (float32) or $-10^4$ (float16). Must ensure $e^{-M}$ underflows to 0.

derivations.consequence: Masking must use large negative values to ensure exact exclusion after softmax.

derivations.title: Parallel training via masking

steps.name: Without masking (naive)
steps.math: To predict position $t$: run model on $1\ldots t-1$. For length $T$: $T$ forward passes. $O(T \times \text{cost})$.

steps.name: With causal masking
steps.math: Run once on full $[1\ldots T]$. Mask ensures position $t$ only attends to $1\ldots t$. All $T$ predictions simultaneously. $O(1 \times \text{cost})$.

steps.name: Correctness
steps.math: At position $t$: output $= \sum_{j \leq t} \alpha_j V_j$. Identical to feeding only $1\ldots t$. Mask simulates sequential in parallel.

derivations.consequence: $T\times$ speedup during training. This is why transformers train faster than RNNs.

derivations.title: Gradient flow through masked positions
derivations.math: Masked: $\alpha_j = 0$. $\frac{\partial \alpha_j}{\partial s_j} = \alpha_j(1-\alpha_j) = 0$. Zero gradient to/from masked positions. Hard information barrier in both forward and backward pass.
derivations.consequence: Masking is architecturally equivalent to positions not existing.

geometric_properties: Causal mask = directed graph: position $i$ receives from $j \leq i$ only
Lower triangular attention matrix = DAG over the sequence
Early positions have less context than later ones
statistical_properties: Early positions (few valid keys) have higher attention entropy
Last position sees everything — typically lowest loss
Per-position loss should decrease with position (more context = better prediction)
numerical_properties: Mask is constant tensor — no params, no compute cost
Precomputable for given sequence length, reused across batches

enables: TRANSFORMER.ENCODER_DECODER — causal mask makes decoder autoregressive
related: TRANSFORMER.TRAINING — teacher forcing relies on masking
